classification.typology_description: A long-lapsed or very new account that has suddenly become active — a classic dormancy-break laundering pattern.

behavioural_indicators.label: Dormancy Signal
behavioural_indicators.description: Low account tenure / dormancy-break signal. Mule median 41 vs legit 64.

behavioural_indicators.label: Activity Flag
behavioural_indicators.description: Elevated transactional activity. Mules score ~22% higher (0.72 vs 0.59).

behavioural_indicators.label: Legitimacy Gap
behavioural_indicators.description: Missing legitimacy indicator. Every confirmed mule has F2082 = 0.

behavioural_indicators.label: Missingness Pattern
behavioural_indicators.description: Activity-count field absent. 82.7% of mules missing it vs 64% of legit.

behavioural_indicators.label: High-Risk Flag
behavioural_indicators.description: F670 = 1 carries a 2.29% mule rate (2.6x the population average).

behavioural_indicators.label: Risk Score
behavioural_indicators.description: Normalised transaction risk score derived from F115.

ai_report:
**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

This report investigates the Alpha-117 account, flagged for high risk with a score of 71.0/100 and a severity level of HIGH. The model risk probability is 0.9720, indicating a strong likelihood of fraud. No match was found in the F3912 fraud registry.

**RISK ASSESSMENT**

The Alpha-117 account exhibits several red flags that contribute to its high risk score. These include an extended tenure (19.0 days), low account standing period (G365D), and activity present after lapse, all of which increase the likelihood of fraudulent behavior.

**KEY FRAUD INDICATORS**

Key indicators contributing to the high risk assessment are:
• Account Tenure (F2956): SHAP +0.310
• Transaction Risk Score (F115): SHAP +0.210
• Legitimacy Indicator (F2082): SHAP +0.170
• Account Standing Period (F3889): SHAP +0.120
• Activity Count (F3043): SHAP +0.090

**MULE TYPOLOGY ASSESSMENT**

The account typology is classified as DORMANT_ACTIVATED with a confidence level of 84%. The matched typology evidence includes:
• Very low / reactivated tenure
• Strong dormancy-break signal
• Activity present after lapse

**ACCOUNT PROFILE ANALYSIS**

Account holder profile:
• Occupation: salaried
• Account standing period: G365D
• Account holder age: 44.0
• Account tenure: 19.0

The account holder's occupation and age do not provide sufficient information to determine the legitimacy of the account.

**RECOMMENDED ACTIONS**

Based on this investigation, we recommend that compliance officers:
1. Conduct a thorough review of the account activity.
2. Verify the account holder's identity and occupation.
3. Investigate the source of funds for transactions.
4. Monitor the account closely for any suspicious activity.

**REGULATORY NOTE**

This report is in accordance with Bank of India's Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Combating the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) regulations, as well as relevant provisions under the Payment and Settlement Systems Regulations, 2007.
